evals.prompt: Bootstrap this ML.NET-style repo. It has build.sh, xUnit tests with a TestBase class using ITestOutputHelper, Contracts.Check for validation, IChannel for logging, and src/ with multiple class library projects.
evals.expected_output: 14 AI-native files generated, all using discovered patterns (build.sh, xUnit, Contracts.Check, IChannel).
expectations: AGENTS.md exists and contains a directory tree of src/ and test/
AGENTS.md contains a real test example using xUnit with ITestOutputHelper, not a fabricated one
AGENTS.md mentions the agent pipeline: plan, implement, test, review, consolidate
.github/copilot-instructions.md references build.sh as the build command, not 'dotnet build'
.github/copilot-instructions.md mentions Contracts.Check as the validation pattern
orchestrator.agent.md has YAML frontmatter with name, description, and tools
orchestrator.agent.md references build.sh for build verification
implementer.agent.md references Contracts.Check for validation and IChannel for logging
test-writer.agent.md specifies xUnit as the test framework and shows TestBase with ITestOutputHelper
security-reviewer.agent.md covers class library threats (deserialization, path traversal), not web API threats
fix-build.prompt.md has mode: agent frontmatter
copilot-setup-steps.yml uses build.sh, not dotnet build
copilot-pre-commit.sh includes format check and secrets scan
No files contain unfilled placeholders like {{PROJECT_NAME}} or [discovered build command]

evals.prompt: Bootstrap this ASP.NET Core 8 Web API project. It uses dotnet build (no custom script), NUnit for tests with [SetUp] methods, FluentValidation, Serilog, and has controllers in src/MyApi/Controllers/.
evals.expected_output: 14 AI-native files generated with web API security model, NUnit patterns, FluentValidation, and Serilog references.
expectations: copilot-instructions.md uses 'dotnet build' since no custom build script exists
test-writer.agent.md specifies NUnit with [Test] attributes and [SetUp] methods, not xUnit
implementer.agent.md references FluentValidation for input validation
implementer.agent.md references Serilog for logging
security-reviewer.agent.md covers web API threats: XSS, CSRF, SQLi, auth bypass
security-reviewer.agent.md does NOT primarily cover class library threats
AGENTS.md directory tree shows src/MyApi/Controllers/
All agent files have valid YAML frontmatter with name field

evals.prompt: This repo already has an AGENTS.md and copilot-instructions.md but no .github/agents/ directory. Add the agent layer without replacing existing files.
evals.expected_output: Agent files created in .github/agents/, existing AGENTS.md and copilot-instructions.md preserved or enhanced.
expectations: Existing AGENTS.md was NOT overwritten or replaced
Existing copilot-instructions.md was NOT overwritten or replaced
.github/agents/orchestrator.agent.md was created
.github/agents/planner.agent.md was created
.github/agents/test-writer.agent.md was created
Agent files reference patterns consistent with the existing AGENTS.md content

evals.prompt: Bootstrap this monorepo that has three .sln files: src/Core/Core.sln, src/Api/Api.sln, and src/Worker/Worker.sln. Each uses MSTest.
evals.expected_output: 14 AI-native files covering all three solutions with MSTest patterns throughout.
expectations: AGENTS.md describes all three solutions (Core, Api, Worker) with their purposes
copilot-instructions.md lists build commands for all three solutions or a unified build approach
test-writer.agent.md uses MSTest attributes ([TestMethod], Assert.AreEqual)
orchestrator.agent.md handles multi-solution build verification
The generated files do not focus exclusively on one solution while ignoring the others
